displayTitle: How Big Is It? Two Ways to Size a Flaw

beats.narration: The question every inspector gets: how big is it? Echo height alone won't tell you: a tall echo is not a big flaw.

payload.title: HOW BIG IS IT?
payload.sub: Echo height alone is not size.

beats.narration: Two families: amplitude comparison against a reference, for flaws smaller than the beam; probe motion, for larger. Flaw versus beam decides the method.
The classic six decibel drop: peak the echo, move until it falls to half height, mark. Repeat the other way. That travel is flaw length.

payload.heading: The 6 dB drop: size by moving
payload.note: AT HALF HEIGHT, BEAM CENTER = FLAW EDGE

beats.narration: Flaw smaller than the beam? The drop method measures the beam and oversizes. Switch to the twenty decibel drop, or tip diffraction for crack height.

chips.t: Small flaw? You measure the beam

beats.narration: So the rule: size the flaw, not the beam. Report length and through-wall height, not one echo.
